paper_title: Materials and Methods of IODP Expedition 303

methods.description: Process for preparing sediment samples from Site U1305 for various analyses, including stable isotope analysis, foraminiferal assemblages, IRD counts, and Mg/Ca analysis.

steps.description: The core was sampled continuously with 2-cm sample spacing over the interglacial periods MIS 7e, MIS 9e, and MIS 11c.
steps.searchable_summary: sample core with 2-cm spacing over specified interglacial periods

steps.description: Samples were soaked in distilled water and left overnight on a shaker to disperse the sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: soak samples in distilled water and shake overnight

steps.description: All samples were wet sieved using a 63-μm-sized sieve.
The coarse fraction (>63 μm) was dry sieved and further used for stable isotope analysis, foraminiferal assemblages, IRD counts, and Mg/Ca analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: dry sieve coarse fraction for further analyses

methods.description: Stable isotope analyses (δ18O and δ13C) were performed on the planktonic foraminifer species N. pachyderma (sinistral) to reconstruct surface ocean physical and chemical properties.

steps.description: N. pachyderma (s) tests were picked every 4 cm from the 150-to 212-μm-sized fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: pick N. pachyderma tests every 4 cm from specified size fraction

steps.description: Tests were measured twice per sample when the abundance allowed.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotope values twice per sample

steps.description: A Finnigan MAT253 mass spectrometer was used for the stable isotope analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform stable isotope analysis using mass spectrometer

methods.description: Analysis of foraminiferal assemblages and IRD counts to understand past ocean conditions and iceberg discharge.

steps.description: Foraminiferal assemblage and IRD counts were performed at every 16-cm resolution through MIS 9e and every 32 cm through MIS 7e and MIS 11c.
steps.searchable_summary: perform assemblage and IRD counts at specified resolutions

steps.description: At least 300 planktonic foraminifera were counted per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: count at least 300 planktonic foraminifera per sample

steps.description: Calculate the coiling ratio of N. pachyderma (s) as the percentage of the sinistral coiling variety.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate coiling ratio of N. pachyderma

steps.description: IRD grains in the >150-μm fraction were also counted.
steps.searchable_summary: count IRD grains in specified fraction

methods.description: Reconstruction of sea surface temperatures (SSTs) using the modern analog technique (MAT) from planktonic foraminiferal assemblages.

steps.description: Use the modern North Atlantic core top planktonic foraminiferal assemblage database compiled by the MARGO Project.
steps.searchable_summary: use MARGO Project database for SST reconstruction

steps.description: Compute modern SST values at sample locations using the World Ocean Atlas (WOA) 98 Sample software.
steps.searchable_summary: compute modern SST values using WOA 98 software

steps.description: Match foraminiferal assemblages at Site U1305 with modern core top database using the program Analog.
steps.searchable_summary: match assemblages with modern database using Analog program

steps.description: Use squared chord distance as the dissimilarity measure.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate dissimilarity using squared chord distance

methods.description: Analysis of Mg/Ca ratios in planktonic foraminifers to estimate past sea surface temperatures.

steps.description: Pick an average of 75 to 90 individuals from the 150-to 250-μm fraction for Mg/Ca analysis.
Crush foraminiferal tests under a microscope and transfer into acid-leached vials.
steps.searchable_summary: crush foraminiferal tests and transfer to vials

steps.description: Clean the crushed foraminiferal tests using a cleaning protocol to remove clays, metal oxides, and organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: clean foraminiferal tests using cleaning protocol

steps.description: Convert Mg/Ca data to temperature estimates using calibration equations.
steps.searchable_summary: convert Mg/Ca data to temperature estimates
